overview: Compares translations between two or three documents, calculating similarity scores (ED-Score and FM-Score) for each text unit. The first document contains the baseline translation; the second (and optionally third) contain translations to compare against it. Outputs HTML reports with per-segment scores and summary statistics, and optionally a TMX document. Also calculates an **Edit Effort Score** useful for measuring MT post-editing effort.

generateHTML.help:
Creates one HTML file per input document listing source text, translation text, and comparison scores.

Also generates a **tab-delimited `.txt` file** with per-entry scores. The output file name is the first document's name plus `.html` (or `.txt`).
notes: Generates both an HTML report AND a tab-delimited text file with scores when enabled.

autoOpen.help: Automatically opens the first HTML result file in the default browser when processing completes. Only applies when HTML output is enabled.

dependsOn.condition: only meaningful when generateHTML is true

genericCodes.help:
Controls how inline codes appear in the HTML report.

- **Enabled**: codes shown as `<1>...</1>` generic placeholders
- **Disabled**: original codes are used where possible

Original codes from some formats can be very long and complicated, making the report harder to read.

dependsOn.condition: only meaningful when generateHTML is true

generateTMX.help: Creates a single TMX document containing all compared translations and their scores across all input documents.

tmxPath.help: Full path of the TMX file to generate. Supports pipeline variables for dynamic paths.
tmxPath.values: Supports variables: `${rootDir}`, `${inputRootDir}`, and locale variables (`${srcLoc}`, `${trgLoc}`, etc.)

dependsOn.condition: only meaningful when generateTMX is true

target2Suffix.help:
Suffix appended to the target language code for document 2 entries in the TMX output. This differentiates translations from different documents.

If left empty, document 1 and 2 will share the same language code and the TMX will only contain the last entry.
notes: If no suffix is provided, both target entries share the same language code and the TMX will include **only the last one**.

dependsOn.condition: only meaningful when generateTMX is true

target3Suffix.help: Suffix appended to the target language code for document 3 entries in the TMX output. Same behavior as the document 2 suffix.

dependsOn.condition: only meaningful when generateTMX is true

caseSensitive.help: When enabled, `"Hello"` and `"hello"` are treated as different, affecting both ED-Score and FM-Score calculations. Disable to ignore case variations in the comparison.

whitespaceSensitive.help: When enabled, differences in whitespace (extra spaces, tabs, line breaks) affect the similarity scores. Disable to normalize whitespace before comparison.

punctuationSensitive.help: When enabled, punctuation differences (e.g., period vs. comma, missing punctuation) affect the similarity scores. Disable to ignore punctuation variations.

document1Label.help: Label used in HTML and TMX reports to identify the baseline translation. Useful for distinguishing sources when reviewing reports.

document2Label.help: Label used in reports to identify the second translation being compared against the baseline.

document3Label.help: Label used in reports to identify the optional third translation.

useDataLog.help: Appends average scores for each document to a persistent log file, allowing you to track comparison results over time across multiple runs.

dataLogPath.help:
Full path to the log file where average scores are appended.

The log is a **tab-delimited UTF-8 file** with fields: timestamp (UTC), base document path, second document path, edit-distance score, fuzzy score.

dependsOn.condition: only meaningful when useDataLog is true

useAltTrans.help:
Uses the target from a specific XLIFF `<alt-trans>` element as the baseline translation for document 1 instead of the main `<target>`.

This enables comparing a linguist's human translation against an MT candidate within the **same XLIFF file** — specify the same file as both document 1 and document 2.
notes: Only works when document 1 is an XLIFF file.
If no matching `<alt-trans>` is found for a given trans-unit, that trans-unit is **excluded** from the report.

altTransOrigin.help: The value of the `origin` attribute on the `<alt-trans>` element to use as the baseline translation.

dependsOn.condition: only meaningful when useAltTrans is true

processingNotes: Input documents **must have the same number of text units** in the **same order** (different events between text units are allowed).

Two scoring metrics are used: **ED-Score** (Edit Distance based on Levenshtein distance) and **FM-Score** (Fuzzy Match based on Sørensen-Dice coefficient with 3-grams). Both range from 0 (completely different) to 100 (identical).
The **Edit Effort Score** (0–100) quantifies post-editing effort: 0 means no changes, 100 means fully rewritten. Calculated as `100 - (avg_ED_by_word + avg_FM_by_word) / 2`.
Neither score is very accurate on **short segments**. Adding a word count step (e.g., Simple Word Count Step) before this step enables word count annotations in the report.
By-word averages weight longer segments more heavily than by-segment averages, better reflecting actual editing effort.

examples.title: MT Post-Editing Effort Measurement
examples.description: Compare machine translation output against post-edited human translation to measure editing effort. Use the same XLIFF file as both document 1 and 2, with `useAltTrans` enabled to extract the MT candidate from `<alt-trans>`.
examples.config:
```yaml
useAltTrans: true
altTransOrigin: BING
caseSensitive: true
whitespaceSensitive: true
punctuationSensitive: true
generateHTML: true
autoOpen: true
document1Label: MT-Bing
document2Label: PostEdit
```

examples.title: Data Log Output Format
examples.description: When `useDataLog` is enabled, average scores are appended as tab-delimited lines to the log file, one per document comparison.
